notes: Forward returns at 5/20/60d horizons overlap across consecutive daily signals; effective independent sample is far below nominal n. Directional evidence only — not grounds for mechanical parameter changes.
v8 telemetry (divergence, hostile_review, regime driver/basis) began accruing mid-June 2026; sections 2-4 deepen with time.
Temporal z baseline excludes the most recent observation; generate-signals consumes the stored baseline next morning (one-day staleness, immaterial at window 60).

tactical.falsifier: A confirmed break below the $257 52w low on rising volume, or LHX cohort lag extending past ~15pp on company-specific bad news (guide cut / program loss), would flip this positive/trim.

positional.falsifier: A B/B print below 0.95 with negative backlog YoY, or a guidance cut confirming the margin erosion is structural, would flip this to neutral/trim.

strategic.falsifier: Confirmation from earnings that op margins are structurally stuck below 12% with no recovery path, or a shift in DoD budget signals toward sequester/peace-dividend, would push this decisively to trim (+30+).

tactical.falsifier: A sustained RSI breakout above 70 with book-to-bill confirmation would flip me to trim; a sharp RSI collapse below 35 on a >5% drawdown day would flip me to buy.

positional.falsifier: A drawdown greater than 15% from highs would flip me firmly to the buy side; conversely a parabolic blowoff extending 20%+ above the 50-day would flip me to trim.

strategic.falsifier: Forward P/E crossing above 45x, OR TSMC+Samsung+Intel all guiding capex cuts / a China revenue collapse would push the score decisively positive (trim); a >15% drawdown pulling forward P/E under 25x would flip it negative (buy).

tactical.falsifier: QUAL factor flipping to negative vs SPY (quality bid rolling over) alongside LIN breaking below its 50-day ($513) would flip this to a positive/trim tactical read.

positional.falsifier: BBB OAS widening sharply (>150bps) or LIN breaking the 200-day with peer spreads blowing out would flip the positional read positive.

strategic.falsifier: Confirmation that ROCE/margin decline is structural (moat erosion) AND price/mix ex-FX turning negative with backlog growth going negative would push strategic firmly positive; conversely a >$1B H2 offtake win with backlog acceleration would flip it negative.

tactical.falsifier: MSFT reclaims the 50-day ($402) with RSI pushing >65 on rising volume (flip toward less buy/neutral as the easy drawdown edge is spent), OR MSFT breaks decisively below the $349 52w low on accelerating volume (flip toward trim as the drawdown thesis breaks down into a downtrend).

positional.falsifier: Azure constant-currency growth prints <22% on the next quarter with capex still rising (thesis impairment → flip to trim), OR MSFT recaptures the 200-day ($441) confirming trend repair (would reinforce, not flip); the sign-flipper is the Azure decel-with-capex-blowout combination.

strategic.falsifier: A decisive Fed pivot / 10Y TIPS breaking back below ~1.8% (real-rate tailwind → reinforces buy) versus Azure CC decelerating below 22% or an adverse OpenAI governance/IP renegotiation (structural moat erosion → flip to trim); the OpenAI partnership unwind or Azure sub-22% print is the sign-flipper.

tactical.falsifier: A sharp 10Y move (>25bps in a session) either direction that ENB reacts to disproportionately would create a fade/buy setup and flip this off zero.

positional.falsifier: Yield spread widening back above ~150bps (via a 10Y drop toward 3.5% or an ENB price pullback to the low-$50s) would flip this to a buy tilt.

strategic.falsifier: Onset of a clear BoC/Fed easing cycle pushing real rates lower, or a yield spread re-widening above 200bps, would flip this to a buy.

tactical.falsifier: A daily close back above the 50-day SMA ($14.97) on rising volume would flip this to a tactical buy.

positional.falsifier: COPX rolling over (copper spot falling below its 50-day) while GLNCY holds — i.e., the leadership flips and the catch-up rationale disappears — would flip this positive.

strategic.falsifier: Evidence the earnings trough is deepening rather than recovering — copper breaking down below key support, Chinese PMI back into contraction, and a cut/suspension to the capital-return program — would flip this to a trim.

tactical.falsifier: Two consecutive daily closes below the $32.84 52w low on rising volume would flip this to a tactical sell — momentum breakdown confirmed, no mean-reversion edge.

positional.falsifier: A decisive break and weekly close below the $32.84 low accompanied by continued net ETF outflows would flip the positional sign — it would confirm the drawdown is trending, not bottoming.

strategic.falsifier: Sustained multi-month ETF net outflows across the complex combined with declining active addresses would flip strategic to a genuine sell — evidence the secular adoption thesis is stalling.

tactical.falsifier: MXN breaking below ~18.5/USD on a Banxico dovish surprise (tactical sell) OR a decisive close back above the $106 50-day on volume (tactical buy).

positional.falsifier: A sustained MXN move past ~18.5/USD or a Mexican volume decline in the next quarterly report would flip this positive (trim); conversely a peso rally toward 16.5 with volume growth would push it more negative.

strategic.falsifier: A regime shift in MXN (sustained trend above ~19/USD driven by fiscal/policy deterioration) or evidence nearshoring is stalling would flip the strategic score positive.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive break/hold above the 50-day SMA ($18.45) on rising volume would flip this to a buy; a break below the 200-day ($15.86) would flip it toward trim.

positional.falsifier: A sustained close below the 200-day SMA ($15.86) combined with WTI under $65 flips this positive/trim; reclaiming the 50-day flips it more negative.

strategic.falsifier: A concrete government action — CEO removal, fuel-pricing intervention below import parity, or a forced capex/dividend-cut announcement — flips this decisively positive/trim; a sustained oil recovery above $80 with dividend policy intact flips it more negative.

tactical.falsifier: SCFI/WCI spot rates print a sharp week-over-week increase (>10%) or a fresh Red Sea escalation headline, confirming the rally is fundamentally driven — that would flip me to a buy-side tactical score.

positional.falsifier: A close back below the 50-day ($12.23) accompanied by GSCPI falling below 0.5 would break the constructive positional setup and flip me to trim-side.

strategic.falsifier: SCFI/WCI freight rates breaking to new multi-year lows combined with the orderbook delivery wave accelerating (fleet growth clearly outrunning demand) would signal the trough is deepening structurally, flipping strategic to trim-side.

tactical.falsifier: RSI closing >72 with a negative XBI 30d rollover, or a sharp reversal back below SMA50 (~$480), would flip this to a trim.

positional.falsifier: XBI 90d rolling back below +10% (funding refreeze) plus a soft next-quarter booking print would flip positional toward neutral/trim.

strategic.falsifier: Confirmed NIH budget cuts plus DHR/Sartorius/Repligen all guiding bioprocessing down again (cycle re-deteriorating, not bottoming), or forward P/E stretching >28x without growth re-accel, would flip strategic to trim.

tactical.falsifier: A confirmed intraday close below the 50-day ($102.6) followed by continued lower lows with rising volume would flip this to a momentum-short (positive score); conversely a sharp reclaim of $110+ confirms the buy.

positional.falsifier: A cRPO deceleration below 18% or negative EPS revision trend on the next print would confirm the down-thesis and flip to trim; a reclaim of the 200-day with positive EPS revisions flips it to conviction buy.

strategic.falsifier: Confirmed cRPO re-acceleration (or holding >20%) with positive EPS revisions on the next earnings print flips this decisively negative (strategic buy); a cRPO print below 18% or op-margin compression from AI costs confirms the trim.

tactical.falsifier: A sharp reversal below the SMA50 (~$500) on rising volume, or VIX spiking >25 with duopoly rolling to a fear discount vs SPY, would flip this to a buy setup.

positional.falsifier: Cross-border volume printing <6% or a negative EPS revision cycle (90d estimates cut) would flip this to a trim — that would mean the pullback is fundamental, not sentiment.

strategic.falsifier: Measurable real-economy merchant displacement by stablecoins (not pilots), OR interchange/network-fee legislation advancing to a floor vote / passing, would flip this from buy to trim.

tactical.falsifier: July 16 print showing procedures below 12% YoY paired with explicit competitor US share-loss commentary, OR transition quantified as material — that flips this from oversold-buy to sell-the-break.

positional.falsifier: Negative EPS revisions (30d/90d turning down) confirming estimate cuts, or I&A growth decoupling below procedure growth for a second quarter — either flips this from accumulate to trim.

strategic.falsifier: Management quantifying the 2027 transition as material (>mid-single-digit % of revenue) OR measurable US procedure-share loss to Hugo/Ottava — either flips strategic to a positive/trim score.
